title: Day 11 Interview Prep
description: Practice event loop, infinite scroll, pagination, and Intersection Observer patterns.

questions.question: Explain the JavaScript event loop in simple terms.
keyPoints: Event loop picks tasks when stack empty
questions.sampleAnswer: JavaScript runs one call stack at a time. Synchronous code runs to completion. Async callbacks queue up — microtasks like Promise.then run before macrotasks like setTimeout. The event loop continuously checks: stack empty? Run microtasks. Then run one macrotask. Repeat.

questions.question: What is the difference between microtasks and macrotasks?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Day 11 core topic — must answer with examples.
keyPoints: Microtasks drain fully before next macrotask
questions.sampleAnswer: Microtasks include Promise callbacks and queueMicrotask. Macrotasks include setTimeout, setInterval, and message events. After sync code, ALL microtasks execute before the next macrotask. So Promise.then runs before setTimeout(0).

questions.question: How does infinite scroll work in React?
questions.sampleAnswer: I place a sentinel div at the list bottom with a ref. IntersectionObserver fires when it enters viewport. Callback calls loadNextPage if not loading and hasMore. Append new items with setItems(prev => [...prev, ...newItems]). Disconnect observer in useEffect cleanup.

questions.question: Walk through output order: sync, Promise.then, setTimeout.
questions.sampleAnswer: console.log sync runs immediately. Promise.then enqueues microtask — runs after sync, before setTimeout. setTimeout(0) is macrotask — runs last. If Promise.then schedules another .then, that nested microtask runs before any macrotask.

questions.question: How do you type Intersection Observer callbacks in TypeScript?
questions.sampleAnswer: Callback receives entries: IntersectionObserverEntry[]. Check entries[0]?.isIntersecting. Ref is useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null). Observer typed as IntersectionObserver from DOM lib. Guard sentinelRef.current before observe.

questions.question: Duplicate items appear after infinite scroll. How do you debug?
questions.sampleAnswer: I check Network tab for duplicate page requests — missing isLoading guard lets observer fire twice. I add guard at top of loadNextPage. If requests are unique but items duplicate, I dedupe by id when appending or verify page increment logic.

questions.question: Compare button pagination (Day 09), infinite scroll (Day 11), and virtualized lists.
keyPoints: Button pagination: explicit, SEO-friendly, simpler state
Infinite scroll: seamless UX, harder deep linking
Virtualization: renders visible rows only — thousands of items
Choose based on UX and data size
questions.sampleAnswer: Button pagination gives explicit page control and shareable URLs — good for search results. Infinite scroll suits social feeds. Neither solves DOM bloat at 10k items — virtualization (react-window) renders only visible rows. I pick based on product UX and list size.

questions.question: User navigates away mid-fetch. Observer still fires. What went wrong?
questions.whatInterviewerTests: Cleanup discipline from Day 08 extended to observers.
questions.sampleAnswer: Missing useEffect cleanup. Observer callback still runs after unmount and may call setState on unmounted component. Fix: return () => observer.disconnect() from effect. Also AbortController.abort() on fetch in cleanup — same pattern as Day 08 race handling.
redFlags: Cannot connect observer to useEffect lifecycle
